title: Services, Units, Boot State, and Logs · Operations Recall
summary: Diagnose a service that is enabled but unavailable by separating desired boot state, current process state, dependencies, configuration, and logs. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a service lifecycle and journal evidence packet after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate service managers, units, dependencies, targets, process supervision, and application readiness.
Use status, is-active, is-enabled, show, list-dependencies, and failed-unit evidence before changing service state.
Query journal fields, units, boots, priorities, time ranges, cursors, and follow mode without losing causal context.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Service Unit Model · changed case
steps.instruction:
review case — lab-worker.service is enabled and active, but readiness failed after configuration revision 5. Query effective unit settings and journal events from this boot before any restart or rollback.

Which action demonstrates Service Unit Model without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for relate service managers, units, dependencies, targets, process supervision, and application readiness.

content.text: lab-worker.service is enabled and active, but readiness failed after configuration revision 5. Query effective unit settings and journal events from this boot before any restart or rollback.

content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Service Unit Model condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: The Open Group Base Specifications Issue 8 — Controls portable shell, utility, process, file, environment, and system-interface semantics.

hints: Name the Service Unit Model precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: lab-worker.service is enabled and active, but readiness failed after configuration revision 5. Query effective unit settings and journal events from this boot before any restart or rollback.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: An enabled service is necessarily running and healthy now.

options.text: An enabled service is necessarily running and healthy now. This does not resolve service unit model · changed case.

For service unit model · changed case, apply Service Unit Model without its stated preconditions, omit the a service lifecycle and journal evidence packet trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Service Unit Model for a service lifecycle and journal evidence packet.

lines.label: evidence

options.text: The Service Recovery record says a service lifecycle and journal evidence packet looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve separate evidence from confidence.

For separate evidence from confidence, apply Service Recovery without its stated preconditions, omit the a service lifecycle and journal evidence packet trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Service Recovery for a service lifecycle and journal evidence packet.
The record names the assumption, applies Service Recovery, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Service Recovery: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — lab-worker.service is enabled and active, but readiness failed after configuration revision 2. steps.why: Retrieving Service Recovery through the concrete a service lifecycle and journal evidence packet case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define restart limits, readiness checks, dependency order, failure escalation, and rollback evidence for a service change.
